author: w3Lb
content: Delivered in commit 71c8a16. undo.max_file_bytes config (default 4 MiB, 0=unlimited) enforced via shared::record_undo_capped across file/script/json modes — oversize files are edited but not recorded, one-line stderr note (ordered even in parallel runs since recording happens on the main thread from outcomes). --no-undo CLI flag (conflicts --undo) + JSON record_undo:false + schema. MEASURED on the 64MiB corpus: default cap trims ~1s off the 0.3.0 baseline mean; --no-undo adds only ~9% beyond the cap — correcting the original hypothesis: the dominant remaining cost vs sed is the engine's per-line String materialization, NOT undo serialization. BENCHMARKS/FAQ/GUIDE/README updated with the honest numbers. Proposal (c) (file-per-entry undo storage) intentionally not done — the cap removes the pain without a storage-format migration; engine streaming for huge files would be the real follow-up if big-file parity ever matters. 11 new tests. 25 suites green, clippy/fmt clean.
